**Challenge OR documented search (per guide Â§7.1):**

**Challenge**: The PR body says "No residuals" and the test-evidence line says "All instances of 8001 changed to 8003," but a scoped residual sweep still finds active runtime substrate in `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/openapi.yaml`:

- `openapi.yaml:77` still tells agents that an existing database can be started externally with `npm run ai:server-memory`.
- `openapi.yaml:81` still recommends `npm run ai:server-memory` for concurrent agents.
- `openapi.yaml:2010` still uses `localhost:8001` in the DB connection error example.

That OpenAPI file is not archive material; it feeds the live MCP tool surface. ADR 0003 says Memory Core no longer manages its own Chroma daemon and depends on the shared Chroma instance instead.
